questions.question: In the following question, select the number which can be placed at the sign of question mark (?) from the given alternatives. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p5035/image006.jpg)
In the following question, select the odd number from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: The sum of the squares of 1st 2 digits is written 25+1= 26 and not 27 4+9=13 4+25=29 1+16=17

questions.question: A series is given with one term missing. Select the correct alternative from the given ones that will complete the given series. F, H, K, M, P, R, ?
questions.explanation: The series is +2, +3, +2, +3, ... F _ H _ _ K _ M _ _ P _ R _ _ U

questions.question: Arrange the words given below in a meaningful sequence. 1. Evaporation 2. Precipitation 3. Waterbody 4. Condensation 5. Rainbow
questions.explanation: The correct sequence is: 31425. Waterbody, evaporation, condensation, precipitation, rainbow.

questions.question: In a certain code language 'DROUGHT' is coded as 'OSDWTIG'. How is 'MANNERS' coded in that language?
questions.explanation: MAN+N+ERS Given wordMANNERS Re-arrangedNAMNSRE +1+2+1 Coded wordNBMPSSE

questions.question: In a certain coded language BIT is coded as 49 and BAT=41 then which of the following will be the code for TAP?
questions.explanation: If we assign position values to alphabets such that A= 1, B=2, C= 3 and so on, then to get the code, the position value of 2nd alphabet is added to the product of the 1st and 3rd position values. B= 2, I= 9, T=20; 2x20+9= 49 B=2, A=1, T=20; 2x20+1= 41 T=20, A=1, P=16; 20x16+1= 321

questions.question: Select the set of numbers that is similar to the following set of numbers. (24, 33, 51)
questions.explanation: The sum of digits of each number in a set is same. In the given set, it is 6 i.e. 2+4 = 3+3 = 5+1 Similarly, in option (b) it is, 3+1= 4+0 = 2+2 = 4 This logic follows only in option (b).

questions.question: In the following question, select the related letters from the given alternatives. VMTH : ZPUG ∷ NCDD : ?
questions.explanation: V+4=Z M+3=P T+1=U H-1=G Similarly, N+4=R C+3=F D+1=E D-1=C

questions.question: Select the word-pair in which two words are related in the same way as are the following word-pair. Unveil: broadcast
questions.explanation: Unveil is to uncover, is to broadcast (similar meaning words) Similarly, token is same as symbolization. While all other pairs have opposite words.

questions.question: Find the next term in the series. 21, 13, 16, 36, 130, ?
If '+' means '÷', '-' means 'x', 'x' means '+' and '÷' means '-', then what will be the value of 3-16x195+15÷42?
questions.explanation: Given expression becomes 3x16+195÷15-42 => 48 + 13 - 42= 19

questions.question: Find the odd one out.
questions.explanation: All other options represent figures of speech. A sonnet is a poetic form.

questions.question: Neelam, who is Deepak's daughter, says to Deepika," Your mother Rekha is the younger sister of my father who is the third child Of Ramlal." How is Ramlal related to Deepika?
questions.explanation: Neelam's father is Deepak. Deepika's mother is Rekha. Deepak is the third child of Ramlal. So Deepak's younger sister Rekha is the daughter of Ramlal. So Ramlal is the father of Rekha and grandfather of her daughter Deepika.

questions.question: Arrange the following words as per the order in dictionary. 1. Mischievous 2. Misappropriation 3. Misconception 4. Misapprehension 5. Miscellaneous
questions.explanation: This is the correct form as per the order in dictionary. Misapprehension> Misappropriation> Miscellaneous> Mischievous> Misconception

questions.question: A mother said to her son, "15 years from now, you will be as old as I was when you were 5 years old". Which one of the following can be the age of the son and mother respectively?
questions.explanation: Let the present age of son and mother be 's' and 'm' respectively. Age of mother when son was born = m - s Age of mother when son was 5 years old = m - s + 5 This quantity, according to the problem statement, is equal to the age of son 15 years from now. m- s + 5 = s + 15 => m = 2s + 10 Only option. (c) can satisfy this equation

questions.question: In each question below are three statements followed by three conclusions numbered I, II and III. You have to take the three given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the three given statements disregarding commonly known facts. Statements: All parks are rinks. Some rinks are charts. All charts are dimes. Conclusions: I. Some dimes are parks. II. Some charts are parks. III. Some dimes are rinks.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/syllogism/image0032515_rinks_parks.jpg) Only III follows
